title: Why Cipher Mining (CIFR) Is Up 14.0% After Landmark AWS AI Infrastructure Deal in Texas
text: The article highlights Cipher Mining's significant stock surge following a major announcement. The company formed a joint entity to develop a 1-gigawatt data center in Texas and secured an approximately US$5.5 billion, 15-year lease agreement with Amazon Web Services. This partnership marks a strategic pivot for the firm. Previously focused on cryptocurrency mining, Cipher is now positioning itself to transform from primarily Bitcoin mining to a significant provider of turnkey space and power for the rapidly expanding AI and high-performance computing sector. The AWS agreement is structured with phased delivery beginning in 2026. However, the article cautions that while this represents substantial growth potential, it also introduces significant execution risk attached to delivering new high-capacity data center projects. Analysts project ambitious targets: the company aims for $696.2 million in revenue and $91.1 million in earnings by 2028, requiring consistent yearly growth. Fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St community range considerably, spanning from $6 to $21.88 per share, reflecting diverse investor perspectives on Cipher's execution capability.
